_guide: This file binds the skill to ONE save and records everything an agent would otherwise have to re-derive every session. Fill in every field during onboarding -- don't leave a placeholder in a live sanctum. Where something is genuinely not yet known, write the literal string "unknown" rather than deleting the key: an absent key reads as 'never checked'; an explicit unknown reads as 'checked, not yet answered'. Remove this _guide key once the real config is written.
farm_id: {{FARM_ID -- the farmId that is YOURS in farmland.xml / vehicles.xml / farms.xml. Almost always 1 in a single-player save, but confirm it by reading farms.xml rather than assuming -- a multi-farm save or a joined MP save may not be 1. Every parser takes --farm-id; this is the value to pass.}}
map: {{MAP_NAME -- from read_career.py. If it's a custom map, include the mod id (e.g. 'FS25_SomeMap.MapSomeMap'), not just the friendly name -- the id is what other tooling needs to match it.}}
economic_difficulty: {{EASY | NORMAL | HARD -- from read_career.py. Re-check this each session if a briefing ever seems to assume the wrong difficulty; it can be changed mid-save.}}
days_per_period: {{INTEGER -- from read_environment.py's `days_per_period` (the live <daysPerPeriod>); cross-check against read_career.py's `planned_days_per_period`. How many in-game days each season lasts -- it paces crop growth and drives the interest-rate x12 scaling (see _note_interest_rate below, F-124). Re-check each session if timing advice seems to assume the wrong pace; it can be changed mid-save.}}
growth_mode: {{INTEGER -- from read_career.py's `growth_mode`. The save's crop-growth mode setting (seasonal growth vs. paused vs. grow-anywhere); it shapes whether planting/harvest-window advice applies at all. Re-check if a briefing assumes seasonal growth that isn't on.}}

notifications._guide: Can this manager put a card on the player's SCREEN mid-session? Needs the AI Farm Manager 25 mod installed and enabled in FS25. Set 'available' at onboarding by LOOKING for the mod zip in the profile's mods/ folder -- never by sending a message and reading the exit code, which cannot tell 'not installed' from 'game closed'. Optional: everything else works without it.

paths._note: Fill in real absolute paths for THIS machine, not example values. On WSL, Windows drives mount at /mnt/<letter>/... -- the skill's default lookup assumes a Linux home and will miss a Windows install entirely, so verify this rather than trusting a default.
paths.install_dir: {{ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_FS25_INSTALL -- the game's install directory. Needed to read BASE-GAME store prices for anything not yet owned, e.g. install_dir/data/vehicles/**/*.xml carries a plain <price> tag per vehicle. Without this, new-equipment prices look unreadable and get wrongly punted to the player.}}
paths.mods_dir: {{ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_MODS_FOLDER -- needed for MODDED store prices (vehicles/placeables shipped in .zip mods) and for this map's own config, e.g. a custom map's farmlands.xml carries pricePerHa and per-parcel price scaling.}}

store_data._note: Leave this whole object out until you've actually located and verified real price files -- don't pre-fill guessed paths. Once verified, record one key per category you've confirmed, e.g. 'vehicles': 'install_dir/data/vehicles', plus a short 'verified' note (date + one concrete example price checked against the save) so a future session trusts it without re-deriving it.

paths.map_config_in_mod: {{unknown until verified -- path to the active map mod's own config XML (e.g. .../config/farmlands.xml) if this is a custom map, for pricePerHa and parcel pricing}}

_note_interest_rate: When this farm carries a loan whose annual rate is known, record it as a TOP-LEVEL key `interest_rate_annual` here (a fraction, e.g. 0.04 for 4% -- NOT a percent; 4.0 reads as 400%). It is derived from farms.xml's finance stats, so verify daysPerPeriod first: it's a straight x12 scale-up only when daysPerPeriod is 1 (F-124). Keep it TOP-LEVEL, NOT nested under notional_budget -- nesting it there made every no-notional-budget farm read a permanent false STALE (F-114). A farm with no loan legitimately has no rate: omit the key rather than seeding a fake one. Remove this _note once the real value (or a deliberate omission) is recorded.

notional_budget._note: Most farms leave this false -- it exists for a farm running a house rule that separates the save's raw/actual cash from some other figure the player wants tracked instead (a cheated save being repaid as debt, a stricter self-imposed budget, etc). If you enable it, this block MUST also record: the formula relating the tracked figure to the save's real number, which file the real number comes from, the date the rule was anchored, and why. state/finances-ledger.md's template explains why this matters: a ledger must never let a derived number be mistaken for the game's own.

house_rules._note: Freeform list of anything THIS farm has agreed to do differently from a default playthrough -- e.g. a self-imposed debt-repayment rule, 'never buy equipment used', 'never sell grain below X/1000L'. This is the enumerated source of truth for house rules; identity/creed.md and identity/decision-making.md point here rather than restating them. Empty is a legitimate starting state. Each entry added later should say what the rule is, when it was set, and why, so a future session enforces it instead of rediscovering or contradicting it. If this list is growing past ~15 entries, that's a signal some are stale or superseded, not a target to hit -- review with the player at a session close and mark or remove ones no longer in effect, the same honesty identity/directives.md applies to closing a directive.
